expectations.text: 결과 구조가 Button/index.tsx 또는 동급 folder/index 규칙을 따른다
expectations.evidence: Output file exists at components/Button/index.tsx, following the folder/index convention.

expectations.text: flat components/Button.tsx 파일이 그대로 남지 않는다
expectations.evidence: No flat components/Button.tsx file exists in the outputs directory. Only components/Button/index.tsx is present. Transcript also confirms: 'Already absent. No action required.'

Lines 13-35 of components/Button/index.tsx: 'const Button = ({ children, variant = "primary", disabled = false, onClick, testId, }: ButtonProps) => { return ( ... ); };' followed by 'export default Button;'

The component never had a dedicated 'label' prop; it uses 'children' as the labeling mechanism. The children prop is preserved in the refactored component (line 6: 'children: ReactNode') and the showcase page continues to pass label text via children: '<Button testId="button-primary">기본 버튼</Button>'.

The component uses 'variant' (not 'tone') to control visual style. The variant prop is preserved with identical type ('primary' | 'secondary'), default value ('primary'), and className logic. Showcase usage is unchanged: '<Button variant="secondary">'.

expectations.text: showcase page의 import가 새 구조에 맞게 정리된다
expectations.evidence: app/showcase/page.tsx line 1: 'import Button from "@/components/Button"' — this path resolves to the folder-based components/Button/index.tsx. No change was needed since the import was already compatible.

expectations.text: useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation 호출이 없다
expectations.evidence: Searched all output files (components/Button/index.tsx and app/showcase/page.tsx). None contain useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, or useMutation.

The folder is named 'Button' (PascalCase), not 'button' (camelCase). Output path: components/Button/index.tsx. The assertion explicitly expects lowercase 'button' but the output uses uppercase 'Button'.

The component is placed at components/Button/index.tsx, not under components/common/button/. The 'common' subdirectory is not used for the Button component. The transcript shows no awareness of this convention requirement.

claims.claim: Both components/Button.tsx and components/Button/index.tsx existed initially as duplicates
claims.evidence: Transcript contradicts itself: 'Initial state' section lists both files, but 'Convention violations identified' #3 says 'The flat Button.tsx was already absent from the file system at inspection time.' Only the folder version actually existed.

claims.claim: Converted from export default function to arrow function pattern
claims.evidence: Output file shows 'const Button = (...) => { ... }; export default Button;' which is the arrow function pattern.

claims.claim: Props preserved exactly: children, variant, disabled, onClick, testId
claims.evidence: ButtonProps interface in output has exactly these five props: children (ReactNode), variant ('primary' | 'secondary'), disabled (boolean), onClick (() => void), testId (string).

claims.claim: No changes needed to showcase page
claims.evidence: The showcase page in outputs is identical to what was described — it uses the same import path and prop API. Import '@/components/Button' resolves to the folder-based index.tsx.

claims.claim: TypeScript: No errors in Button or showcase files
claims.evidence: Cannot verify TypeScript compilation from static file inspection alone. No metrics.json or build log available.

suggestions.reason: The component has no 'label' prop — it uses 'children'. This assertion is misleading and passes vacuously. Consider asserting 'children prop behavior is preserved' or checking that the prop interface matches the original exactly.
The component has no 'tone' prop — it uses 'variant'. This assertion is misleading and passes vacuously or by interpreting 'tone' as 'variant'. Consider using the actual prop name to avoid ambiguity.

This conflicts with the first assertion which expects 'Button/index.tsx' (PascalCase). The eval has contradictory expectations — assertion 1 expects PascalCase 'Button' while assertion 9 expects camelCase 'button'. Clarify which convention the project actually follows.
No assertion checks whether the className/styling logic was preserved correctly. A refactor that accidentally drops or alters the Tailwind classes would pass all current assertions.

eval_feedback.overall: Two assertions (label, tone) use prop names that don't exist in the component, making them ambiguous. Two assertions (folder-camelCase vs folder-structure) contradict each other on casing convention. Consider aligning assertion terminology with the actual component API and resolving the PascalCase vs camelCase conflict.
